section_miscategorizations.text: Any difficulty with swallowing? Any abdominal pain? Nausea or vomiting, changes in bowel movements? And how long have you had this problem?
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are clinician questions rather than patient-reported history. In a written note, unanswered clinician queries belong to neither HPI nor Findings; including them in HPI as if they were part of the patient narrative is misleading documentation structure.

section_miscategorizations.text: We do know that caffeine and caffeinated beverages will do that, even sodas.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is counseling/education about triggers and management, which belongs in the Plan rather than in the symptom/history narrative.

section_miscategorizations.text: It's reassuring that you're not having any difficulty swallowing, though.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a clinician synthesis of a negative symptom (no dysphagia), which functions as a clinical finding rather than patient narrative; it should be documented as part of exam/review findings, not embedded in HPI dialogue.

section_miscategorizations.text: From what you've told me, it sounds like you've had a several-year history of Gerd, which is the medical term for acid reflux, and recently your symptoms have been poorly controlled with the omeprazole, 40 milligrams twice daily.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is explicit clinician synthesis of the history into a diagnostic impression and control status, which belongs in the Assessment rather than in the HPI.

section_miscategorizations.text: So the first thing I'd try is changing your diet because these foods can exacerbate your Gerd, even though you're taking the ppi. Stress is another trigger that we need to address. High stress can cause worsening of your symptoms. So working on that may help with your Gerd.
section_miscategorizations.reason: These are management recommendations and counseling (dietary modification and stress management) and should be in the Plan, not in the HPI.

section_miscategorizations.text: Additionally, we can consider trying a different ppi since the only one you've tried up to this point is omeprazole. If you're okay with this, we can switch your omeprazole to nexium. I will send a prescription for nexium 40 milligrams twice daily to your pharmacy on file.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a concrete treatment decision and prescription change, which is part of the Plan rather than the HPI.

section_miscategorizations.text: I suspect that she likely has Gerd with underlying functional etiology as well.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is a diagnostic impression and differential consideration, which belongs in the Assessment, not in the Plan.
